prompt:
Create a striking vertical 9:16 cinematic product-poster for NuLumin Bio-Sciences 'Cellular Research'. Aesthetic: vivid, bright, eye-catching luxury pharma editorial — saturated and bold, NOT dark or moody. Inspired by the booth scatter shots (bright color background with floating vials) but elevated.

THREE PEPTIDE VIALS (HEROES): Epithalon 30mg (REFERENCE 1), MOTS-C 20mg (REFERENCE 2), NAD+ 500mg (REFERENCE 3) — each with its signature blue cap and the EXACT NuLumin label artwork preserved (italic NuLumin wordmark on top, 'BIO-SCIENCES' subtext, italic peptide name, bold blue mg dose, 'Manufactured by NuLumin' line, small side info block). Do NOT alter any label text.

VIAL POSE — DYNAMIC FALLING: the three vials look as if they have been gently tossed into the air and are caught mid-fall by the camera. Each vial is at a different rotation and angle (one tumbling forward, one nearly vertical, one tilted backward), suspended at eye-level / center of the canvas. Subtle implied motion — slight gravitational descent feel without aggressive motion blur (labels stay sharp and readable). Vials are SMALL — roughly 22–28% of canvas height each — clustered loosely in the middle third with generous space at top and bottom and ample side margins. The composition feels alive and weightless, premium and editorial.

BACKGROUND — BRIGHT AND THEMATIC: the background color must MATCH the exact blue of the peptide vials' caps and accent text shown in REFERENCES 1, 2, and 3 — a vivid saturated mid-tone sky/azure blue (NOT teal, NOT navy, NOT royal blue, NOT cyan). Pull the blue directly from the vial caps in the references and use that as the dominant background color. A smooth gradient of that same blue (slightly brighter at the center, slightly deeper at the edges) — clean and luminous, like a luxury skincare ad. The overall background and the vial caps should read as the SAME family of blue so the composition feels color-coordinated. Behind the vials, integrated softly into the gradient: tasteful abstract cellular imagery — translucent glowing white stylized cells with visible nuclei and faint organelles, like a microscopic field of bioluminescent cells drifting across the background. The cells should feel like elegant editorial decoration (not literal medical illustration) — soft glow, fine line art, varying sizes, KEPT WITHIN THE CENTER 60% OF THE CANVAS WIDTH with clear empty margins on the left and right. Do NOT push the cells or any decorative motifs near the left or right edges of the frame — leave generous edge space for canvas scaling. Subtle bokeh light particles in the air to add atmospheric depth, also kept toward the center.

VIAL LIGHTING: bright soft directional studio light from upper front, glowing blue ambient bouncing through the glass and liquid, gentle specular highlights on the blue caps, soft drop shadows below each vial reinforcing the floating-mid-fall feel. The vials look photorealistic and integrated — clearly part of the bright blue scene.

CATEGORY TYPOGRAPHY (smaller and centered — must NOT extend near the edges): Title at the top of the canvas:
  • 'CELLULAR' set in Playfair Display, bold display weight, all caps, clean white, generous letter-spacing — hero word.
  • 'RESEARCH' set just below in thinner Playfair Display italic, smaller, refined editorial pairing.
  • The title occupies AT MOST the center 50–55% of the canvas width — significant empty side margins on the left and right of the title. Do NOT scale the title to span full width. Smaller, refined, museum-poster proportions.
  • Soft glow halo behind the type so it lifts from the bright blue background.
  • Optional thin minimal hairline rule under the title.
No other text anywhere — no taglines, no subtitle, no marks beyond the vial labels.

COMPOSITION FOR 4000×9600 CANVAS: leave a substantial safe margin around the edges — title should not touch the top, vials should not touch the sides or bottom. The whole composition is centered, leaving the image scalable.

Final output: photorealistic, sharp focus on the labels, vivid bright blue palette with elegant cellular motifs, vials caught mid-fall at eye level, premium luxury pharma editorial vibe, 9:16 portrait, 4K, no extra text, no watermarks, no additional vials.
